2606.13007.title: scLLM-DSC: LLM-Knowledge Enhanced Cross-Modal Deep Structural Clustering for Single-Cell RNA Sequencing
2606.13007.paper_summary: Clustering is fundamental to scRNA-seq analysis, serving as a cornerstone for identifying cell populations and resolving tissue heterogeneity. However, existing methods focus on mining numerical statistical patterns, suffering from semantic agnosticism by neglecting the intrinsic biological functions encoded by genes. While Large Language Models (LLMs) offer promising semantic capabilities, their direct adaptation to cell clustering is hindered by the structural mismatch between generative pre-training objectives and discriminative downstream tasks. To bridge this gap, we propose scLLM-DSC, a novel LLM-Knowledge Enhanced Cross-Modal Deep Structural Clustering framework. Diverging from data-driven paradigms, scLLM-DSC establishes a semantically-grounded representation by synergizing two views: a Knowledge-Driven Semantic View derived from NCBI gene priors and contextualized Cell2Sentence embeddings, and a Structure-Aware Topological View extracted via a graph-guided encoder. Crucially, we introduce a cross-modal contrastive alignment mechanism to enforce consistency between biological semantics and transcriptomic features within a unified latent space. Extensive benchmarks demonstrate that scLLM-DSC significantly outperforms eleven state-of-the-art baselines in clustering accuracy.
2606.13007.paper_summary_zh: 聚類對於 scRNA-seq 分析至關重要，作為識別細胞群體和解決組織異質性的基石。<lb/>然而，現有的方法專注於挖掘數值統計模式，因忽視基因所編碼的內在生物功能而遭受語義無知。<lb/>雖然大型語言模型 (LLMs) 提供了有前景的語義能力，但由於生成預訓練目標與判別下游任務之間的結構不匹配，其直接適應於細胞聚類受到阻礙。<lb/>為了彌補這一差距，我們提出了 scLLM-DSC，一種新穎的 LLM-知識增強跨模態深度結構聚類框架。<lb/>與數據驅動的範式不同，scLLM-DSC 通過協同兩個視角建立了一個語義基礎的表示：一個基於 NCBI 基因先驗的知識驅動語義視角和上下文化的 Cell2Sentence 嵌入，及一個通過圖引導編碼器提取的結構感知拓撲視角。<lb/>至關重要的是，我們引入了一種跨模態對比對齊機制，以強化生物語義與轉錄組特徵在統一潛在空間中的一致性。<lb/>廣泛的基準測試顯示，scLLM-DSC 在聚類準確性方面顯著超越了十一個最先進的基準。
2606.13007.author: Ping Xu et.al.
2606.13007.authors: Ping Xu, Pengjiang Li, Tian Du, Zaitian Wang, Jiawei Gu, Ziyue Qiao, Pengfei Wang, Yuanchun Zhou
